decision.summary: Suitable as DSXU-owned senior coding discipline: assumptions, tradeoffs, simplicity/no-second-runtime, surgical scope, and success criteria. Not suitable as a copied Skill/plugin/runtime.

rows.absorptionDecision: Absorb as DSXU Action Contract discipline, not as a new skill runtime.
rows.hardGap: Assumptions, ambiguity, tradeoffs, and pushback are not yet mandatory visible-state fields before nontrivial mutation.
rows.nextExecution: Extend existing Action Contract and work-state projection with assumption/tradeoff/success-criteria evidence.

rows.dsxuOwner: Product Core Guard / owner boundary / replace-delete review
rows.absorptionDecision: Translate simplicity into DSXU-owned no-second-runtime, no speculative layer, no duplicate owner discipline.
rows.hardGap: Current guard protects product core paths, but does not yet score speculative abstraction or owner duplication on every change.
rows.nextExecution: Add speculative-abstraction and duplicate-runtime smell checks to existing owner/review evidence, not a new linter runtime.

rows.dsxuOwner: Code-mode surgical loop / blast radius / post-mutation verification
rows.absorptionDecision: Keep existing DSXU surgical loop, strengthen changed-line-to-user-request obligation.
rows.hardGap: DSXU has scope fences and post-mutation evidence, but final report does not always prove every touched file maps to request/owner/test.
rows.nextExecution: Add touched-file scope ledger to final patch report and work-state timeline.

rows.absorptionDecision: Keep DSXU verification ladder; require explicit success criteria before multi-step mutation and public claim.
rows.hardGap: Success criteria exist in several gates, but are not uniformly required at task start for all complex coding paths.
rows.nextExecution: Add success-criteria field and missing-success-criteria guard to Action Contract/work-state/final report.

rows.absorptionDecision: Do not expose Karpathy/Claude/Cursor names as DSXU product feature names; use neutral internal analysis only.
rows.hardGap: External files contain brand/product wording and install flows that are not DSXU-owned.
rows.nextExecution: Public README/docs must describe DSXU senior-coding discipline in DSXU terms and cite no borrowed brand claims.

rows.dsxuOwner: MCP / Skill Registry only as optional external inspiration
rows.absorptionDecision: Do not install as primary DSXU Skill. It is a guideline source, not a runtime capability.
rows.hardGap: Adding it as a skill would create priority/conflict ambiguity and product-surface branding risk.
rows.nextExecution: Convert into DSXU-owned Action Contract policy pack; no plugin install, no CLAUDE.md import, no Cursor rule copy.

publicClaimBoundary: May claim DSXU has a source-truth/scope/verification discipline only after code/test/live evidence is bound.
Must not claim DSXU includes Karpathy/Claude/Cursor plugin behavior.
Must not use simplicity as an excuse for lightweight or incomplete verification.
